STAT / ASAP / SPECIAL SCHEDULING
⚡ STAT Orders
  • Contact the department for approval to add to the schedule, then send an Add-On email to the DL. "STAT" must be on a valid order (5 criteria). Ask if it has STAT.
  • If no open slot, obtain approval to double book (except THAllen) or refer to another location. Document the approving person's name in RAD notes.
  • Inform the caller of potential delay. Send an Add-On email to the facility and call the radiology department for a heads-up.
📋 ASAP / Same Day / Next Day
  • For same-day scheduling, obtain department permission. Document the approving person's name in RAD Notes and send an Add-On email. 
  • If it's 2:00 p.m. or later and the caller wants the next day before 10:00 a.m., obtain department permission. Document the approving person's name in RAD Notes and send an Add-On email. 
  • If it's 2:00 p.m. or later and the caller wants the next day after 10:00 a.m., schedule the appointment, call the department, and send an Add-On email.
  • Valid order (5 criteria) must be faxed in with NO failed MN, an Add-On email sent, and the procedure must not require authorization (Ultrasound). For modalities requiring authorization, ASAP is typically 48 business hours unless permission is granted by the entity.
♿ Special Needs
  • If answered yes, note the required assistance in RAD notes: wheelchair, lift, interpreter, limited range of motion, cane, etc.
  • For lift or interpreter, send an Add-On email.
🏥 Nursing Home / SNF / LTAC
​
  • ​Please click here to view a list of facilities with an existing agreement. 
  • If the facility you are scheduling for is not on the list, it is possible an agreement needs to be signed.
  • Schedule 4-5 business days out to obtain an agreement if needed. Enter the patient's insurance information, then put facility name and phone number in the appointments notes. 
  • Send an email to the entity by using the Add On Email DL stating, "Patient may possibly be coming from a SNF or LTAC, and an agreement may need to be in place prior to arrival. The patient's insurance information is entered however, if billing the facility, please enter specialty billing."

CT ABDOMEN
Modality: CT Body Region: ABDOMEN
Warning
IF ORDER STATES WITH ORAL CONTRAST ONLY , TRANSCRIBE CT ABD WO & IN COMMENTS DOCUMENT "ORAL ONLY".
Protocol Note
PROTOCOLS: LIVER/SPLEEN/KIDNEYS/LIVER PROTOCOL/PANCREATIC PROTOCOL/KIDNEY (RENAL) MASS PROTOCOL/ADRENAL PROTOCOL.
Important
Contrast indication must be on order.
Note
IMPORTANT: When we receive an order for an "abdomen ONLY" and the diagnosis is "right lower quadrant pain or left lower quadrant pain", we should make an attempt to the physicians office to clarify/confirm a pelvis is not required.
